article_title: Peregrine Raises $250 Million at $6.8 Billion Valuation as Its Government-Data Search Platform Reaches 400 Agencies
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Peregrine Raises $250 Million at $6.8 Billion Valuation as Its Government-Data Search Platform Reaches 400 Agencies
content_preview.summary: Peregrine's Series D nearly triples its valuation in 15 months as it scales real-time government-data search to more than 400 agencies.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Peregrine Technologies, a company that builds real-time search and operations software for government agencies, has raised $250 million in a Series D round at a $6.8 billion valuation, according to the [company's announcement](https://peregrine.io/resources/peregrine-technologies-raises-250-million-series-d-at-68-billion-valuation). The financing was led by Fifth Down Capital, with participation from Sequoia Capital, OG Venture Partners, Goldcrest Capital, XYZ Ventures, and Godfrey ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (604 words) with a standard Overview / What We Know / Background and Context / What We Don't Know layout. Technical and financial specifics are precise and the piece reads as neutral business reporting rather than a press-release rewrite; it deliberately balances the company's own framing against independent (Forbes) reporting and a named civil-liberties critic.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four source snapshots were fetched at 200 and read from disk; none required a WebFetch fallback. source-0.html.gz (peregrine.io company release) — CONFIRMS the $250M Series D, $6.8B valuation, the full investor list (Fifth Down Capital lead per PR Newswire framing; company release lists them as existing investors), Noone (co-founder/CEO) and Rudolph (co-founder/CTO), 400+ agencies serving 125M+ people, doubled customer base, 450+ employees, offices in SF/DC/NYC/Toronto/London, the event deployments (Super Bowl, Grammys, World Series, Kentucky Derby, Academy Awards), 8 of 11 World Cup host cities, Fairfax County 13-minute suspect ID, Manatee County hurricane response, SAVE KC 18% violent-crime decline, and the Noone privacy/civil-liberties quote verbatim, plus the Andy Spellman (Fifth Down Capital) quote. source-1.html.gz (PR Newswire) — CONFIRMS 450 employees, Toronto/London, 18%, 13 minutes, Fairfax, Manatee, SAVE KC, 125 million, 400 agencies, Noone, Rudolph, Spellman surname, the 'technical talent and rigor' quote, and the privacy/civil-liberties quote. DOES NOT CONTAIN any reference to a prior $2.5B Series C valuation or a 15-month timeframe (see concerns). source-2.html.gz (Pulse 2.0) — CONFIRMS the 2018 founding, the 'unify information across siloed systems ... secure and permission-aware environment' description, and the doubled customer base, all as cited. source-3.html.gz (Forbes, Aug 2024, Thomas Fox-Brewster) — CONFIRMS verbatim: Noone ran Palantir's Special Operations business and 'spent years in the Middle East working with the U.S. military, using disparate intelligence datasets to help identify ISIS members in Syria'; the 'Enter a name or address ... any police dataset imaginable — for a match' search-tool description; the Beryl Lipton (EFF) quote 'These types of companies... are inherently going to have a hard time protecting privacy'; and the safeguards paragraph (constantly updated granular audit log, access controls, license-plate lookups requiring a case number/clear reason, facial recognition not allowed at all).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific except one traces to a cited snapshot. The single exception: the summary ('nearly triples its valuation in 15 months') and the Overview's second paragraph ('The raise nearly triples the valuation the company carried at its Series C, which was $2.5 billion just 15 months earlier') are attributed to PR Newswire, but no cited source — PR Newswire, the company release, Pulse 2.0, or the 2024 Forbes profile — contains a prior $2.5B Series C valuation or a 15-month timeframe. The only 'tripled' figure in any source is Forbes's unrelated 2023 revenue growth ('$3 million to $10 million'). This is a single, self-contained unsourced/misattributed specific that a corrections note can honestly cover; it does not appear in the headline, and the core news (the $250M raise, $6.8B valuation, 400-agency scale) is fully sourced and stands without it.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The civil-liberties dimension is handled correctly: the EFF criticism is explicitly attributed to Beryl Lipton via Forbes and quoted verbatim, and the company's countervailing safeguards (audit logs, license-plate restrictions, facial-recognition prohibition) are presented alongside it. No editorializing, no AI self-reference, no loaded language.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Peregrine coverage in the archive; the only pre-existing 'peregrine' match is an unrelated lunar-lander article. Not a duplicate.
concerns: The summary and the Overview lead's second paragraph state Peregrine's Series C valuation was $2.5B '15 months earlier' and that the Series D 'nearly triples' it, attributing this to PR Newswire. No cited source contains a prior valuation figure or timeframe; the claim is unsourced and misattributed. Filed as a correction rather than a reject because it is a single recoverable specific outside the headline, and the article's central facts are fully verified.

Two of four sources (peregrine.io and pulse2.com) are off the source allowlist. peregrine.io is the company's own release (appropriate as a primary/company-statement source and clearly attributed as such); pulse2.com is a trade-press corroboration. Judged acceptable editorially given PR Newswire and Forbes anchor the independent reporting. Allowlist not modified.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Substantively strong, neutral business/civil-liberties reporting with the vast majority of specifics verified verbatim against the snapshots. The one unsourced specific — the $2.5B Series C valuation and 'nearly triples in 15 months' framing, misattributed to PR Newswire — is recoverable via a public corrections record and does not undermine the headline or the core news. Off-allowlist sources are a normal editorial warning, not a blocker.
